title: A Supernova That Helps Us Learn About Dark Energy
summary: Astronomers have found a special supernova from 10 billion years ago that might help solve a big mystery about the universe. Dark energy is a force that makes the universe grow faster, but we don't know what it is. This supernova is very bright because a galaxy's gravity bends its light, making it look bigger. The light splits into multiple images, and each image shows the supernova at a slightly different time. By studying these images, scientists can measure how fast the universe is expanding. This could help them understand dark energy better and fix a problem where different measurements of the expansion rate don't match. The discovery is exciting because it uses gravity to see faraway things and might answer big questions about space.

keywords.explanation: a huge explosion of a star that makes it very bright
a mysterious force that makes the universe expand faster
a force that pulls things together and can bend light
everything that exists in space, including stars and galaxies
when the universe gets bigger over time
pictures or views of something, like the supernova seen from Earth

questions.question: What is the supernova from 10 billion years ago helping scientists study?
What force bends the light from the supernova to make it look brighter?
How many images of the supernova can astronomers see because of gravity?
What does dark energy do to the universe?
Why is each image of the supernova slightly different?
What problem might this supernova help solve about the universe's expansion?
options: We don't know how old it is

background_read: Dark energy is a mysterious force that scientists think makes the universe expand faster, but they don't know what it is. A supernova is a big explosion of a star that shines brightly. Gravity is a force that pulls things together and can bend light, which helps us see faraway objects like this supernova. The universe is everything in space, and it's getting bigger over time. Astronomers use telescopes to study these things and learn more about how the universe works.
Article_Structure: WHO: Astronomers and researchers like Dr. Daniel Perley and Jacob Wise
WHAT: Found a rare supernova from 10 billion years ago that might help understand dark energy
WHERE: In space, with light traveling to Earth from a distant galaxy
WHY: To solve the mystery of dark energy and fix problems in measuring how fast the universe expands

perspectives.description: This supernova is a new tool for astronomers to study dark energy and the universe's expansion.
